q: Yesterday afternoon we ____ to the city centre to buy my brother a birthday present. (go)
The old museum near the river ____ its doors to the public in 1925. (open)
When the bus arrived, all the tired passengers ____ off and hurried towards the station. (get)
My grandmother ____ me an interesting story about the city's busy old market. (tell)
While I was ____ across the square, a street musician began to play the guitar. (walk)
They were ____ for the right bus stop when it suddenly started to rain heavily. (look)
At eight o'clock last night the traffic ____ moving very slowly through the busy streets. (be)
We have lived in this noisy neighbourhood ____ I was a little child.
The shopping centre has been open ____ more than twenty years now.
I have already ____ all the famous tourist sights in our beautiful capital city. (visit)
When my parents were young, they ____ to live in a small town far from here.
There ____ to be a cinema on this corner, but they knocked it down last year.
The new library is much ____ than the old one, with hundreds more books. (big)
This is the ____ skyscraper in the whole country, and you can see it from miles away. (tall)
The underground is almost as ____ as a taxi, but it is far cheaper.
If it ____ tomorrow, we will visit the art gallery instead of the park. (rain)
If you take the early train, you ____ arrive in the city before nine o'clock.
Look at those dark clouds over the towers, it is ____ to rain very soon.
I ____ meeting my friends outside the train station at half past four this afternoon.
There is too ____ traffic in the city centre during the busy morning rush hour.
There aren't ____ free parking spaces near the crowded shopping mall on Saturdays.
The pavement was so crowded that there wasn't ____ room to walk comfortably.
That is the friendly police officer ____ helped us find our way to the hotel.
The square ____ the festival takes place is decorated with thousands of lights.
In a big city you ____ to be careful when you cross the busy main roads.
It's getting dark, so we ____ take a taxi back to the hotel rather than walk.
You can catch a bus, a tram or a train at this busy place where many routes meet, called a ____.
People walk safely along the raised path beside the road, which is called the ____.
The two sides of the river are joined together by a long stone ____ that cars drive across.
We waited at the corner until the traffic ____ turned green and we could safely cross.
Last summer a terrible storm ____ across the valley and knocked down dozens of old trees.
The farmers ____ all their crops because of the long drought that lasted for months. (lose)
When the volcano erupted, thick clouds of ash ____ the entire sky for several days. (cover)
We ____ a beautiful rainbow above the waterfall after the rain finally stopped. (see)
While we were hiking up the mountain, it suddenly started to ____ heavily and we got soaked.
The wind ____ blowing so fiercely that the sailors could not control the boat. (be)
The children were ____ in the lake when the lifeguard warned them about the rising tide. (swim)
While the thunder ____ rumbling in the distance, the campers quickly packed up their tents. (be)
Scientists have ____ that the climate has changed dramatically since the last century. (prove)
It has not ____ a single drop of rain here for almost three months now. (rain)
Have you ever ____ a tornado with your own eyes, or only on the news? (see)
There ____ to be a thick forest here, but they cut all the trees down years ago.
This river ____ to flood every spring, but now a new dam controls the water.
The Pacific is the ____ ocean in the world, far larger than the Atlantic. (deep)
Today the weather is much ____ than it was yesterday, so we can finally go outside. (warm)
In the desert the nights can be almost as ____ as the days are hot.
If the temperature ____ below zero tonight, the lake will freeze completely by morning. (drop)
If it keeps snowing like this, the roads ____ definitely be blocked by tomorrow.
According to the forecast, it is ____ to be extremely foggy along the coast tomorrow morning.
Look at those dark clouds gathering on the horizon, it ____ going to pour down any minute.
There was so ____ pollution in the city that we could hardly breathe the smoky air.
Although the region gets plenty of sunshine, it does not receive ____ rainfall to grow rice.
There were ____ many leaves blocking the drain that the whole street flooded after the storm.
The Amazon, ____ is the largest rainforest on Earth, produces a huge amount of our oxygen.
That is the remote village ____ the famous explorer was born before he sailed around the world.
You really ____ to wear a thick coat outside because the wind chill is dangerously low today.
The sky looks threatening, so you ____ take an umbrella in case it starts to rain.
A long period of unusually dry weather, when little or no rain falls, is called a ____.
A violent storm with a very strong spinning column of wind is called a ____.
By the time we reached the summit, the fog had already ____ and we could see for miles. (lift)
Last Saturday our team ____ the final match by three goals, so everyone celebrated for hours. (win)
The athlete ____ off her trainers before she stepped onto the running track. (take)
When the referee blew his whistle, the players immediately ____ playing and walked off. (stop)
My older brother ____ to the stadium by bus because his bicycle had a flat tyre. (go)
The two teams ____ for over ninety minutes before anybody managed to score a single goal. (play)
While the goalkeeper was watching the crowd, the striker ____ the ball straight past him. (kick)
At six o'clock yesterday the swimmers ____ training hard in the freezing pool. (be)
The coach was shouting instructions while the young gymnasts were ____ on the mat. (practise)
While I was tying my laces, my teammates were ____ towards the changing rooms. (run)
I have ____ tennis since I was seven years old, and I still love every match. (play)
My cousin hasn't finished his fitness training ____, so he can't join the league.
Our team has ____ already won three matches this season. (win)
There ____ to be a basketball court behind our school, but they built a car park there.
My grandfather ____ to run marathons when he was younger, though now he only walks.
Running a marathon is far ____ than a short sprint around the playground. (hard)
Out of all the players in our club, Daniel is the ____ defender we have ever had. (good)
The new striker is almost as ____ as a professional, even though he is only fourteen.
If our goalkeeper trains every day, he ____ definitely improve before the championship.
If it ____ heavily tomorrow morning, the referee will cancel the football tournament. (rain)
Look at those dark clouds over the pitch; I think the match ____ going to be postponed.
Next weekend my whole family ____ travelling to the capital to watch the boxing final.
There were so ____ spectators in the stadium that we could hardly find our seats.
The young swimmer drank ____ water during the race that he felt slightly sick afterwards.
Our team didn't have ____ players to start the game, so we had to borrow two.
The marathon runner who finished first was the woman ____ trains at our local gym.
This is the stadium ____ the national team plays all of its most important home matches.
You ____ to wear special boots if you want to play rugby safely on a muddy field.
If you feel exhausted after training, you ____ drink plenty of water and rest properly.
The person who watches a football match and makes sure the players follow the rules is the ____.
The crowd of people who come to a stadium to watch and cheer for a team are the ____.
Last weekend we ____ a fantastic film about a young musician who became famous overnight. (watch)
The orchestra ____ a beautiful melody just before the lights went down in the concert hall. (play)
My older brother ____ to the cinema three times last month because he loves action movies. (go)
The famous singer ____ on stage for almost two hours during her amazing live concert. (sing)
Although the ending was sad, I ____ the film very much and would happily watch it again. (enjoy)
While the film was playing, my little sister was quietly ____ popcorn next to me. (eat)
When the power suddenly went off, the band ____ rehearsing their newest song in the studio. (be)
At nine o'clock last night I ____ downloading the soundtrack from my favourite movie. (be)
I have wanted to learn the guitar ____ I was seven years old.
My parents have ____ that documentary about jazz musicians twice already this year. (see)
Have you ____ listened to a whole album by that legendary rock band?
My grandfather ____ to play the violin in a small village band when he was young.
People ____ to buy music on CDs, but now almost everyone streams songs online instead.
This new comedy is much ____ than the boring film we saw last Friday. (funny)
In my opinion, the soundtrack is the ____ part of the entire movie. (good)
The classical concert was not as ____ as the loud rock festival we attended in summer.
If the tickets ____ cheaper, we will definitely go to the concert tonight. (be)
If you listen carefully to the lyrics, you ____ understand the meaning of the song.
Hurry up, the film ____ going to start in just a couple of minutes!
Next Saturday my friends and I ____ watching the new superhero movie at the cinema.
There wasn't ____ space in the cinema, so we had to sit in the very front row.
How ____ people came to watch the live band perform in the park last night?
The music at the party was far ____ loud, so nobody could hear each other talk.
That is the talented actor ____ won an award for his role in the historical drama.
This is the recording studio ____ my favourite band made their very first album.
You really ____ listen to this album because the melodies are absolutely incredible.
We ____ to buy our concert tickets early, otherwise they will sell out completely.
A song that becomes extremely popular and that many people love is often called a ____.
The words of a song, which the singer performs, are together called the ____.
There aren't ____ good films showing at our local cinema this week, unfortunately.
Last weekend my cousins ____ to our house for a big family lunch. (come)
My grandfather ____ up in a small village before he moved to the busy city as a young man. (grow)
When I was younger, my brother and I often ____ over silly things like the TV remote. (fight)
My parents first ____ each other at university more than twenty years ago. (meet)
My parents ____ a wonderful holiday to the seaside for the whole family last summer. (plan)
While my mum was cooking dinner, my little sister ____ a picture of our whole family. (draw)
My cousins were laughing loudly because their dad ____ a funny story about his childhood. (tell)
This time last year, my grandparents ____ travelling around Europe to visit distant relatives. (be)
My aunt has lived next door to us ____ I was a baby.
I have ____ told my best friend about the surprise party for my sister.
My older brother has ____ to live abroad, so the house feels very quiet now. (go)
My best friend has been my neighbour ____ three years now, and we do everything together.
When my dad was a teenager, he ____ to spend every summer at his grandmother's farm.
My sister and I ____ to argue all the time, but now we are really close friends.
My younger cousin is much ____ than me, even though he is only ten years old. (tall)
Out of all my relatives, my grandmother tells the ____ stories about the past. (interesting)
My twin brother is almost as ____ as me, so people often confuse us.
If my parents ____ home early tonight, we will watch a film together.
If you are kind to your younger siblings, they ____ always look up to you.
Next weekend we ____ going to visit my grandparents in the countryside for their anniversary.
My sister is so excited because she ____ meeting her cousins at the airport tomorrow morning. (meet)
There were so ____ relatives at the wedding that I couldn't remember all of their names.
My grandmother always cooks ____ much food for family dinners that we take some home afterwards.
We don't have ____ chairs in the dining room for the whole family to sit down together.
My aunt, ____ lives in Australia, sends us photos of her children every month.
That is the cosy little house ____ my grandparents have lived for over forty years.
You really ____ phone your grandmother more often, because she misses you a lot.
Children sometimes ____ to help their parents with the housework at the weekend.
A ____ is the brother of your mother or your father.
Your ____ are the people you are related to, such as aunts, uncles and cousins.
